title: BlackSky Technology (BKSY) Valuation After $99M Air Force Contract
text: Contract Details: BlackSky Technology secured a multiyear, sole-source U.S. Air Force Research Laboratory contract valued at up to $99 million. The agreement focuses on designing advanced optical payloads and developing space-based intelligence capabilities. Stock Performance: The announcement coincides with impressive share price momentum. BlackSky's stock has risen 47.23% over 30 days and 67.10% year-to-date, with a one-year total return approaching 4x. Valuation Disconnect: The stock trades at $34.79 as of April 2026, creating divergent valuation perspectives: Analyst View (Bearish): The most-followed analyst narrative pegs fair value at $27.63, suggesting the stock is 25.9% overvalued at current prices. This assessment assumes step-function increase in recurring imagery and analytics revenues in 2025 and beyond. DCF Model (Bullish): Simply Wall St's discounted cash flow analysis suggests fair value of $47.38, implying the stock trades at a 26.6% discount based on future cash flow projections. Key Takeaway: Investors face conflicting signals: earnings-based valuations signal caution, while cash flow models suggest upside potential. The company's reliance on government contracts and heavy constellation spending present ongoing risks despite the new contract win.
